prose:
Method provenance:
Property: Frontier orbital energies and HOMO–LUMO gap of H2O (gap reported here)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF
Geometry provenance: single-point on user-supplied geometry /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz (not re-optimized at DFT)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0 (neutral), 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software/back-end: PySCF (version as bundled with chemkit engine)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 7 cycles; integrity.trustworthy = true; no warnings reported by the engine.

Headline result:
HOMO-LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV (HOMO −8.6037183 eV, LUMO +0.33695037 eV) at B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input geometry, gas phase.

Live log & files: Calculation executed via chemkit skill `frontier-orbitals` with command recorded in the JSON (see cli_invocation). Output JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqfd0ijtk/live.json. A live .out log was streamed during the run (path emitted at job start).
